## Story 03101
- **Weight:** 4
- **Channel:** @MattDAvella
- **Source:** yt_transcript_masterclass_matt_davella
- **Video ID:** 

I struggled to stay consistent with healthy habits for years until I discovered the 2-day rule. The rule is simple. Never allow yourself to skip two days in a row. This rule helped me put on 20 lbs of muscle over a few years, build a workout habit that I stuck with for a decade, and it might even add years to my life, unless I get hit by a bus first. So, let's first talk about what makes rules so effective. You see, our brains are really good at thinking, but they are terrible at remembering. A system like this removes the need for constant decision-m. Before this rule, I'd make excuses. I had a busy schedule. I had finals. I was hung over. But once I committed to never skipping two days in a row, everything changed. Even if I just did 10 minutes of cardio, I kept the habit alive. And this doesn't just apply to fitness. This rule can be used for anything. Writing, meditating. If you have the time, this rule is great. If you're a busy parent like me, you might want to set the bar a little bit lower. Maybe it's the 3-day rule instead.

---

## Story 03102
- **Weight:** 4
- **Channel:** @MattDAvella
- **Source:** yt_transcript_masterclass_matt_davella
- **Video ID:** 

If there's one thing that I've learned from moving in with my wife years ago, my amazing, beautiful, and talented wife, it's that sometimes you just can't get a moment to yourself. And so, if you've ever felt happier alone, you aren't alone. Studies have shown that just 15 minutes alone each day can improve your mood, boost productivity, and even strengthen your relationships. Brain scans show that when we're alone, our default mode network activates, meaning better problem solving and fresher ideas. That's why most of your best ideas happen when you're in the shower. Even prairie vos, one of the few mammals that mate for life, show increased bonding after time apart. So, if you need space, just tell your partner, "I'm doing this for us." Good luck with that, by the way. That's not to say that we don't need connection, friendship, and love. We do. We need all those things, but we also need to prioritize time alone.

---

## Story 03103
- **Weight:** 4
- **Channel:** @MattDAvella
- **Source:** yt_transcript_masterclass_matt_davella
- **Video ID:** 

have you ever heard of the goldilocks rule it can teach you everything that you need to know about why you give up when tackling a new project or skill if you want to reach peak cognitive focus you want to work at a level just outside of your comfort zone when author james clear talks about the goldilocks rule he often relates it to tennis you don't want to play against serena williams because you're going to get annihilated you'd get so disheartened that you'd want to smash your racket like mcenroe but you also don't want to play against a five-year-old because you get bored to death you need to find your personal sweet spot experts say that your sweet spot is about four percent higher than your level of competence not too easy not too difficult just right

## Story 03104
- **Weight:** 4
- **Channel:** @MattDAvella
- **Source:** yt_transcript_masterclass_matt_davella
- **Video ID:** 

I only have one pair of pants and I almost never wash them. Sounds kind of gross, but hear me out. Years ago, I read an article that said you shouldn't wash your jeans after every use. At first, I thought this was nonsense, but I tested it out and after months of wearing the same pair of jeans, nothing happened. No bad smell, and no one ran away screaming. Wow, it's so great to finally meet you. I never washed my pants. Oh, do you want to smell them? Yeah. Great. Awesome. Yeah. on. Let me just take my pants off so it's not weird. Even the president of Levis's swears by this practice. These have yet to see a washing machine. I know that sounds totally disgusting. A good pair of denim doesn't really need to be washed in a washing machine except very very infrequently or rarely. Turns out washing jeans too often wears them out faster, wastes water, and honestly, it's just unnecessary. Less laundry, fewer decisions. I'm in. Sure, sometimes my pants need a spot clean or a legitimate wash after a really sweaty day or a day of traveling, but for the most part, this simple shift has made my life just a little bit simpler.

---

## Story 03105
- **Weight:** 4
- **Channel:** @MattDAvella
- **Source:** yt_transcript_masterclass_matt_davella
- **Video ID:** 

We tried minimalism with kids. Well, I guess technically minimalism with a baby. It was way harder than we thought. We are parents. This it happened. My god. Wow. Actually, you look worse than me. The first challenge, pulling down all the recommended items down to the essentials. We still ended up with 100 new things by the time our first son was born. Holy While we had to buy a lot of new things, there were a few helpful practices that we did implement. One, a capsule wardrobe. We kept Frankie's clothes simple. Just a few identical zip-up onesies, all machine washable and dryer friendly. Two, the one bin rule for toys. If a new toy comes in, an old one goes out. This one's going to be a lot harder as he gets older. Three, setting boundaries for gifts. We let family and friends know that we prefer experiences over stuff. Four, smart storage and organization. We used storage bins under the bed for baby gear and organized essentials like bottles, pump parts, solid food supplies neatly into an appropriate cabinet so we didn't drown in chaos. Five. Question every purchase. Before buying anything, we ask, "Do we really need this thing? Is it going to simplify our life? Will it make our life simpler or more complicated?" Turns out the answer was often no. So, can you be a minimalist with kids? Yes, you definitely can. But maybe your kids won't be.

---

## Story 03106
- **Weight:** 4
- **Channel:** @MattDAvella
- **Source:** yt_transcript_masterclass_matt_davella
- **Video ID:** 

I'm a minimalist. My wife isn't. So, I convinced her to try minimalism for a week. It didn't go so well. What are you doing? Oh, I'm sorry. Do you want one? No, I'm good. Thanks, D. First up, the closet. You ready for this? Yeah, let's do it. I'm ready. Natalie packed away 99% of her wardrobe, leaving only 33 items. Unlike me, she didn't really see the value in wearing the same thing every day. Next, digital clutter. Her desktop was a disaster. Her inbox had over 11,000 unread emails. And yes, she still uses Yahoo Mail. This desktop is horrifying. How long has it been like this? Forever. Oh, forever. It's really not that bad. I think you're being dramatic. We backed up our files, organized everything, and amazingly found an actual wallpaper behind all those icons. I don't think she wanted to admit it, but this definitely put her mind at ease. Then the beauty products. She unearthed her secret horde of skincare and makeup products, condensing it all into one bag, and somehow still found room for her glitter eyeshadow. I honestly don't even know what that is. The final boss, cooking condiments. I tried to get rid of some. She refused, stating, "I use every single one of these. I surrendered." Yay. At the end of the week, did she convert to minimalism? Not a chance. But I appreciate the fact that she entertained my lifestyle for a short period.

- **Video ID:** 

I took cold showers for 30 days here's why you might want to try it yourself over the past decade researchers have discovered a number of benefits to cold therapy from reduced inflammation improved metabolism and lower stress levels but the biggest reason why I think you should start taking cold showers is because they can teach you everything you need to know about self-development stepping into a cold shower goes against every Instinct in your body it requires you to embrace what author Julian Smith calls the Flinch oh man I don't want to do this in life we face flinches all the time it could be giving a live talk starting a business or having a difficult conversation with your partner these are moments where we often Flinch at an opportunity for growth but if you want to live a meaningful life you need to repeatedly embrace the Flinch and one of the best ways to practice is jumping into a freezing cold shower you'll come up with all of the excuses for why this is dumb why you don't need to prove yourself to anyone why this is nothing like the challenges that you'll face in the real world but then again maybe that's the Flinch talking

## Story 03108
- **Weight:** 4
- **Channel:** @MattDAvella
- **Source:** yt_transcript_masterclass_matt_davella
- **Video ID:** 

I swap lives with a night owl for one week. I'm an early bird and my friend Sarah is a night owl. So, for one week, we swap routines to see if we could learn anything from each other's schedules. At first, Sarah struggled with the early mornings. I'm exhausted. But she quickly noticed one major benefit, having a distraction-free window for deep work. Meanwhile, I found late nights strangely freeing. With no emails or incoming messages, I was able to focus without interruptions. But there were some major downsides for both of us. Our energy levels and sleep both took a major hit. Another late night for me. Almost 1:00 a.m. I am so done with this. I can't wait to get back to my structure and my routine. Science backs up the idea that our sleep schedules are more genetic than we think. According to sleep researcher Matthew Walker, our chronoype, whether we're naturally early or late risers, is hardwired. Are you a morning type, evening type, or somewhere in between? You have no choice. If you're an evening type, you're an evening type. It's not just a matter of willpower. By the end of the experiment, neither of us had fully converted. Ultimately, the best schedule is the one that works for you. [Music]

---

## Story 03109
- **Weight:** 4
- **Channel:** @MattDAvella
- **Source:** yt_transcript_masterclass_matt_davella
- **Video ID:** 

I tracked my mood every single day for a year because apparently I needed a spreadsheet to tell me if I was happy. Each night I rated my day on a scale from minus1 to plus two, hoping to find patterns in my happiness. At first, it was really difficult to tell because my mood changes constantly, sometimes within the same day, sometimes within the same hour. But as I continued to do this experiment, I started to dial in on a system that worked for me. January 6th, spent 6 hours trying to screw eight holes into a wall. Score minus one. March 20th, ate two donuts, played Last of Us. Score plus one. October 13th, got Natt pregnant. Score plus two. And after a year, the results were eye opening. I had 69 amazing days, 169 good days, 98 fine days, 25 bad days, and four awful days. Overall, a pretty good year. But the real insight, the things that made me happiness weren't big achievements. They were much simpler. quality time with the people I love, doing things just for fun, and working just enough. Happiness isn't about eliminating bad days. It's about learning from them. And sometimes tracking the details is the only way to see the bigger

---

laziness is a skill like tricking your partner into holding an empty cup of coffee training your dog to fetch a cold beer or this brilliant son of a but for those times when laziness just won't get the job done here are three things that will help you get inspired to take action number one change your physical state whether it's taking a cold shower going for a run or hitting the gym when you force yourself to change your state your mood will follow along two change up your routine go to a different cafe find a different route to work jump on a moving freight train and see where it takes you don't do that adding novelty to your day can help you shift your perspective and get you feeling excited again and three get inspired sometimes all you need is to watch a moving film take in a work of art or even listen to bohemian rhapsody when we consume art that inspires us it motivates us to take action hey by the way my habits course is now open for enrollment so if you want to learn all of my rules strategies and mindsets for building habits for life go to slowgrowth.com simple habits or click the link in the description below this video

## Story 03111
- **Weight:** 4
- **Channel:** @MattDAvella
- **Source:** yt_transcript_masterclass_matt_davella
- **Video ID:** 

I timed every single thing I did for 30 days. Brushing my teeth, eating, working, even going to the bathroom. After being really unproductive for months, I wanted to try to figure out a way to increase my output and take better control of my time. I experimented with a dozen apps to get started and settled on one called Toggle to help me track everything that I do in a given day. The results, I realized I was only working 20 hours per week. This is just like way worse than I possibly thought it could be. If I had a boss, I'd probably be fired by now. The reason is because I ran this experiment in the first year of becoming a parent. So, a significant amount of my time was going towards my family, and I had a hard time prioritizing work. Not exactly a bad thing, but going forward, this is helping me get more intentional and helping me shift my behavior a little bit. But also, I spent 4 hours on the toilet this month, so maybe I can steal some time from those tasks as well. Tracking my time has helped me to become more accountable to how I'm spending my time every single day.

---

these are five self-help books that changed my life first the obstacle is the way by Ryan holiday no matter what you're pursuing in life especially if you're ambitious you will face serious challenges along the way and as Ryan puts it these challenges aren't roadblocks they are the path to growth second deep work by Cal Newport this book reaffirmed the idea that attention is our most valuable resource and getting time alone is what leads to our best work stop Doom scrolling put your phone down and find time every day to focus on what matters most the third book I would teach you to be rich by RIT Sadi this book made me rethink how I approach money it's okay to spend big on what you love as long as you cut mercilessly on what you don't the fourth book is all about relationships mainly intimacy she comes first by Ian Kerner I'm not going to explain this one any further I think you get the idea the title of the book alone might change your life and finally number five the book that changed my perspective on life man search for meaning by Victor Frankle it's a powerful reminder that meaning not Comfort is what keeps us going one of my favorite quotes life is never made unbearable by circumstances but only by lack of meaning and purpose

## Story 03115
- **Weight:** 4
- **Channel:** @MattDAvella
- **Source:** yt_transcript_masterclass_matt_davella
- **Video ID:** 

I walk 10,000 steps every single day for a month. Why? Because despite lifting weights regularly, I barely do any cardio, and my daily step count is embarrassingly low. The challenge seemed pretty simple. Walk more, feel better. But what I learned went beyond just numbers on a step tracker. At first, it was tough. I found myself pacing my living room late at night, desperately trying to hit my goal. I got 10,0 steps. 10,0 I ever achieved that. But over time, I made small changes that made a big difference. Taking calls while walking, choosing stairs over the lift, and exploring Sydney's beautiful hiking trails. The result, I felt more energized. I burned more calories, and gained a new appreciation for simply moving more every day. You don't need to have an intense cardio routine every week to live a healthier life. Sometimes it just takes one small step. Okay, maybe 10,000.

---

- **Source:** yt_transcript_masterclass_matt_davella
- **Video ID:** 

a lot of people think that New Year's resolutions don't work but behavioral scientists disagree let's talk about the Fresh Start effect what the Fresh Start effect is it's our tendency to be extremely optimistic about our capacity to change at moments in our lives that feel like Fresh Starts or New Beginnings the classic example of this is New Year's and New Year's resolutions but if you look around you can find Fresh Starts everywhere ever every Monday we're a little bit more optimistic and more likely to pursue our goals we see the same pattern when we look at the start of a new semester for students we feel like at the beginning of these new Cycles in our lives we've turned the page on who we are and when you look at the data it's clear that Fresh Starts make a huge difference if we encourage someone to begin pursuing a goal and we highlight a fresh start date on the calendar in front of them they're more excited and more likely to say yes on those fresh dark dates so whether it's a new year or a new week now might be the best time for a fresh start
